title: Bug with modulo in CXXRTL when operands intersect
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.29+11 (git sha1 0b05a6425, gcc 7.5.0-6ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os), also reproduced in
Yosys 0.29+11 (git sha1 f7ffd12bf, gcc 7.5.0-6ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Hi there!

I noticed that the modulo operation sometimes provides wrong results in CXXRTL when there is an intersection between the operands.

#### Design

For example, this design, supplied with `in_data=0x216231b1f16e9e8`:
```
module top(
    input logic [63:0] in_data,
    output logic [63:0] out_data
);
    assign out_data = in_data[41:6] % in_data[63:28];
endmodule
```

Some valuations of `in_data` yield a correct result and seem to compute faster than faulty valuations.

When there is no intersection between the operands, it seems that the calculation is correct, for example:
```
module top(
    input logic [63:0] in_data,
    output logic [63:0] out_data
);
    assign out_data = in_data[63:32] % in_data[31:0];
endmodule
```

#### Testbench

The testbench can look like this: `tb_cxxrtl.cc`:

```
#include "top.cpp"

int main() {
    cxxrtl_design::p_top top;
    uint64_t in_data=0x216231b1f16e9e8;
    top.p_in__data.set<uint64_t>(in_data);
    top.step();
    uint64_t out_val = top.p_out__data.get<uint64_t>();
    std::cout << "Out data: " << std::hex << out_val << std::endl;
}
```

#### Commands

```
yosys -p "read_verilog -sv top.sv; write_cxxrtl top.cpp"
g++ -g -O3 -std=c++14 -I `yosys-config --datdir`/include tb_cxxrtl.cc -o tb
./tb
```

Thanks!
Flavien

### Expected Behavior

Output value: `0x35522118`

### Actual Behavior

Output value: `0x90beaf8`
code_blocks:
yosys -p "read_verilog -sv top.sv; write_cxxrtl top.cpp"
g++ -g -O3 -std=c++14 -I `yosys-config --datdir`/include tb_cxxrtl.cc -o tb
./tb
